title: aws-kms integration tests: no signature verification
description: integration_sign_and_verify_ecdsa and integration_sign_and_verify_rsa_pss only assert !sig_bytes.is_empty(). They never cryptographically verify the signature against the public key. A bug in ecdsa_der_to_raw_p256 (e.g. off-by-one in the DER parser producing syntactically valid but cryptographically invalid output) would not be caught. Compare to local-signing which does full round-trip verification using p256/rsa verifying keys. Fix: add p256 and rsa as dev-dependencies to secretx-aws-kms, decode the public key DER returned by public_key_der(), and verify the signature using the appropriate verifier.
